title: Bed in Summer (from A Child's Garden of Verses)
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
note: Verbatim — the complete three-stanza poem 'Bed in Summer', one stanza per spread. A real board-scale rhyming poem to contrast with the synthetic board-book fixture: where the synthetic one is max-repetition opposites, this is genuine AABB iambic verse with richer vocabulary ('candle', 'winter'). Exercises the decodability + prosody path at the smallest scale.
prosodyNote: Engine detects dominant meter 'iambic' at consistency ~0.64 (real iambic tetrameter) and an AABB-leaning rhyme scheme — the only board-scale fixture that surfaces a named meter.
